title: centaurea castellana
intro: Centaurea castellana, also known as the Castilian cornflower, is a flowering plant species in the aster family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. Consuming Centaurea castellana has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving digestive health, as well as potentially exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its extracts may also be found in dietary supplements and topical creams.
